summary: Excellent front‑of‑board tape of a hot Southern California run, highlighted by a monster Other One and stellar Garcia guitar work.
review: The recording is a crisp, well‑balanced soundboard capture that lets every instrument breathe – especially Jerry’s lead tone, which sits right up front. The show itself is a late‑80s high point with standouts like Bertha, Dire Wolf, Althea, Red Rooster, Lost Sailor / Saint Stephen and an unforgettable Other One that many reviewers call the best of the decade. The band’s energy, Phil’s “earthquake” riffs during the jam, and Weir’s rhythm work on Greatest Story Ever Told round out a performance that fans repeatedly describe as dynamite.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews are overwhelmingly positive, praising both the musical performance (numerous standout songs and exceptional playing) and the technical quality of the front‑of‑board tape.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑board capture with clear instrument separation, crisp drums, and Garcia’s guitar prominently in the mix; vocals are a bit buried but still intelligible. No reported dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A three‑show Southern California run that builds momentum; first set delivers solid roots numbers, second set features a space jam after Lost Sailor/ Saint Stephen leading into a soaring post‑drums Other One, and the encore adds an unusual Women Smart → Johnny B. Goode finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his slide work, Red Rooster solo, and overall guitar tone – described as “God‑like” and front‑center in the mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for his exuberant “earthquake” riff during the Other One jam; listeners note that his bass is clearer on this tape than on other sources.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar shines on Greatest Story Ever Told, earning specific commendation.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards are well balanced and do not swallow Garcia’s guitar; reviewers comment on good separation of keyboards from the rest of the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers (Bill & Mickey) are clearly defined; pre‑drums is noted as well played, and post‑drums jams receive high praise.
